verses.1: On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women came to the tomb, bringing the spices they had prepared.
verses.2: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
verses.3: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
verses.4: While they were puzzling over this, suddenly two men in radiant apparel stood beside them.
verses.5: As the women bowed their faces to the ground in terror, the two men asked them, “Why do you look for the living among the dead?
verses.6: He is not here; He has risen! Remember how He told you while He was still in Galilee:
verses.7: 'The Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and on the third day rise again.'”
verses.9: And when they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the Eleven and to all the others.
verses.10: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told this to the apostles.
verses.11: But their words seemed like nonsense to them, and they did not believe the women.
verses.12: Peter, however, got up and ran to the tomb. And after bending down and seeing only the linen cloths, he went away, wondering to himself what had happened.
verses.13: That same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
verses.14: They were talking with each other about everything that had happened.
verses.15: And as they talked and deliberated, Jesus Himself came up and walked along with them.
verses.16: But their eyes were kept from recognizing Him.

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “What are you discussing so intently as you walk along?”

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: They stood still, with sadness on their faces.

verses.18: One of them, named Cleopas, asked Him, “Are You the only visitor to Jerusalem who does not know the things that have happened there in recent days?”

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: He asked. “The events involving Jesus of Nazareth,“ they answered. “This man was a prophet, powerful in speech and action before God and all the people.

verses.20: Our chief priests and rulers delivered Him up to the sentence of death, and they crucified Him.
verses.21: But we were hoping He was the One who would redeem Israel. And besides all this, it is the third day since these things took place.
verses.22: Furthermore, some of our women astounded us. They were at the tomb early this morning,
verses.23: but they did not find His body. They came and told us they had seen a vision of angels, who said that Jesus was alive.
verses.24: Then some of our companions went to the tomb and found it just as the women had described. But Him they did not see.”

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “O foolish ones, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!

26.text: Was it not necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then to enter His glory?”
26.speaker: Jesus

verses.27: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, He explained to them what was written in all the Scriptures about Himself.
verses.28: As they approached the village where they were headed, He seemed to be going farther.
verses.29: But they pleaded with Him, “Stay with us, for it is nearly evening and the day is almost over.” So He went in to stay with them.
verses.30: While He was reclining at the table with them, He took bread, spoke a blessing and broke it, and gave it to them.
verses.31: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized Jesus—and He disappeared from their sight.
verses.32: They asked each other, “Were not our hearts burning within us as He spoke with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?”
verses.33: And they got up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem. There they found the Eleven and those with them, gathered together
verses.34: and saying, “The Lord has indeed risen and has appeared to Simon!”
verses.35: Then the two told what had happened on the road, and how they had recognized Jesus in the breaking of the bread.

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: While they were describing these events, Jesus Himself stood among them and said,

verses.37: But they were startled and frightened, thinking they had seen a spirit.

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “and why do doubts arise in your hearts?

39.text: Look at My hands and My feet. It is I Myself. Touch Me and see—for a spirit does not have flesh and bones, as you see I have.”
39.speaker: Jesus

verses.40: And when He had said this, He showed them His hands and feet.

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: While they were still in disbelief because of their joy and amazement, He asked them,

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “Do you have anything here to eat?”

verses.42: So they gave Him a piece of broiled fish,
verses.43: and He took it and ate it in front of them.

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “These are the words I spoke to you while I was still with you: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about Me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms.”

verses.45: Then He opened their minds to understand the Scriptures.

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “This is what is written: The Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,

47.text: and in His name repentance and forgiveness of sins will be proclaimed to all nations, beginning in Jerusalem.
47.speaker: Jesus

48.text: You are witnesses of these things.
48.speaker: Jesus

49.text: And behold, I am sending the promise of My Father upon you. But remain in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high.”
49.speaker: Jesus

verses.50: When Jesus had led them out as far as Bethany, He lifted up His hands and blessed them.
verses.51: While He was blessing them, He left them and was carried up into heaven.
verses.52: And they worshiped Him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
verses.53: praising God continually in the temple.

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
